title: 命案目睹记
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\136-命案目睹记-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
A man discusses his wife, Edie, a former member of the Women's Auxiliary Air Force, noting that her father's assets were left to Alexander. During lunch preparations, Alexander and West return to the kitchen, where Lucy prepares gravy and Yorkshire pudding. Alexander mentions that a Scotland Yard officer is visiting, though Uncle Harold is angry about the ongoing murder case. Mr. Wimbush informs Aunt Emma that Inspector Craddock from Scotland Yard is now in charge of the investigation. Wimbush explains that the victim was likely a foreigner from London, prompting Emma to suggest the woman might be French, a thought that visibly unsettles her.

During lunch, Cedric treats the murder as a morbid joke, while Harold is offended by the incident, Emma is worried, and Alfred is lost in thought. Following the meal, Inspector Craddock and Inspector Bacon question Cedric. Cedric explains he returned from the Balearic Islands after receiving an electric telegram from Emma. He claims he returned on December 21st and left on the 27th. When asked if the victim had a connection to the estate, Cedric denies any knowledge. After the questioning, Inspector Bacon expresses his dislike for Cedric, labeling him a self-righteous "artistic type" and a "loose cannon." Bacon further criticizes Cedric's disrespectful clothing and suggests that Cedric is the type of person capable of strangling a woman without remorse.
